So total backward steps are 2 + 5 = 7 steps. So I'm 7 steps behind the starting point in the direction I was facing. Next, take 5 steps right. Now, since I'm always facing forward, my right would be the right side relative to my facing direction. So if I'm facing forward, moving right would be to the right side. So 5 steps to the right. Then take 9 steps right. Again, same direction as before because I'm still facing forward. So total right steps are 5 + 9 = 14 steps to the right. So now, I need to figure out if after all these movements I'm back where I started. Let's imagine the starting point as coordinates. Let me set up a coordinate system where the starting point is (0, 0). Let's assume forward is the positive y-direction, and right is the positive x-direction. So when I move backward, that's negative y-direction. When I move right, that's positive x-direction. So starting at (0, 0). First, 2 steps backward: each step is 1 unit. So moving -2 in y-direction: (0, -2). Then 5 steps backward: another -5 in y, so total y is -2 -5 = -7. So position is (0, -7). Then 5 steps right: +5 in x, so (5, -7). Then 9 steps right: +9 in x, so total x is 5 +9 =14. Final position is (14, -7). So the starting point is (0, 0), and the final position is (14, -7). So unless there's some rotation or turning involved, which the instructions don't mention, I'm not back to the start.
